hero.title: Image Converter
hero.tagline: Convert image(s) between JPG, PNG, WebP, AVIF, BMP, GIF, and ICO entirely in your browser. No upload required — fast, private, and free.

hint.text: Would you like to crop your image before converting your image?

whatIs.heading: What is Image Converter
whatIs.body: The Image Converter lets you change the format of one or more images entirely in your browser. Select source files, pick a target format, and download the converted results — no server upload required.

howWorks.heading: How conversion works
howWorks.body: Each source image is decoded in the browser, drawn to an offscreen canvas, and then re-encoded in the chosen output format. Conversion quality and file size depend on the source image and the target format's compression characteristics.

formats.heading: Supported formats
formats.jpgDesc: JPEG — Lossy compression, great for photos, widely supported.
formats.pngDesc: PNG — Lossless compression with transparency support.
formats.webpDesc: WebP — Modern format with excellent size/quality ratio.
formats.avifDesc: AVIF — Next-generation compression, best quality per byte.
formats.gifDesc: GIF — 256-color indexed format for simple graphics.
formats.icoDesc: ICO — Windows icon format, multiple size options.

faq.heading: FAQs
faq.q1: Q: Are my images uploaded to a server?
faq.a1: A: No — all conversion happens locally in your browser.
faq.q2: Q: Can I convert multiple images at once?
faq.a2: A: Yes — select multiple files and convert them all at once.
faq.a3: A: Only for formats that support it (PNG, WebP, AVIF). Converting to JPG removes transparency.
faq.q4: Q: Why is AVIF not supported in my browser?
faq.a4: A: AVIF requires a modern browser (Chrome 85+, Firefox 93+). Use WebP as an alternative.

howItWorks.step1: Select one or more images by dragging them in or clicking to browse.
howItWorks.step2: Choose the output format from the format selector buttons.
howItWorks.step3a: Click Convert to process the image instantly in your browser.
howItWorks.step3b: Download your converted image with the Download button.
howItWorks.step4: You can also convert multiple images at once by selecting more than one file.

dropZone.text: Drag & drop images here, or click to select
dropZone.hint: Supports JPG, PNG, WebP, AVIF, GIF, BMP, and more

popup.previewHint: Alt+Scroll to zoom · Drag to pan

format.label: Convert to:
format.jpgDesc: Best for photos. Smaller file, lossy compression.
format.pngDesc: Best for graphics with transparency. Lossless quality.
format.webpDesc: Modern format. Smaller than JPG & PNG with great quality.
format.avifDesc: Next-gen format. Best compression. Chrome & Firefox recommended.
format.bmpDesc: Uncompressed bitmap. Lossless, large file. Max compatibility.
format.gifDesc: 256-color format. Best for simple graphics, not photos.
format.icoDesc: Windows icon format. Choose which sizes to include.

formats.jpgDesc: Best for photos. Smaller file, lossy compression.
formats.pngDesc: Best for graphics with transparency. Lossless quality.
formats.webpDesc: Modern format. Smaller than JPG & PNG with great quality.
formats.avifDesc: Next-gen format. Best compression. Chrome & Firefox recommended.
formats.bmpDesc: Uncompressed bitmap. Lossless, large file. Max compatibility.
formats.gifDesc: 256-color format. Best for simple graphics, not photos.
formats.icoDesc: Windows icon format. Choose which sizes to include.

guide.title: Why Image Formats Matter (And How to Convert Images the Right Way)
guide.intro: You try to upload an image… and suddenly: "File format not supported", image won't open on another device, file size is too large. These issues usually come down to one thing: image format.

whatIs.heading: What Is Image Conversion?
whatIs.body: Image conversion means changing an image from one format to another (for example PNG → JPG or HEIC → JPG). The image content stays the same, but file size, quality, and compatibility can change.

why.heading: Why Do Image Formats Even Exist?
why.body: Different formats exist because they serve different purposes: performance (smaller file size), quality (more detail), and compatibility (works everywhere). One format rarely fits all situations.

whenToConvert.heading: When Do You Need to Convert Images?
whenToConvert.item1: Upload Errors: Some platforms accept only specific formats.
whenToConvert.item2: File Size Too Large: Convert heavy formats like PNG to JPG/WebP to reduce size.
whenToConvert.item3: Device Compatibility: Convert HEIC from iPhones to JPG for wider support.
whenToConvert.item4: Web Optimization: Modern sites prefer WebP for smaller files and faster loading.

formats.heading: Most Common Image Formats (Quick Guide)
formats.jpg: JPEG (JPG) — Best for photos — small files, no transparency
formats.webp: WebP — Modern — smaller size with good quality
formats.heic: HEIC — Used by iPhones — efficient but limited support

bestPractices.heading: Best Practices for Converting Images
bestPractices.item1: Choose format by use case — WebP for web, JPG for photos, PNG for graphics.
bestPractices.item2: Avoid repeated conversions — always convert from the original.
bestPractices.item3: Understand lossy vs lossless: JPG is lossy, PNG is lossless.
bestPractices.item4: Balance quality and size — pick a middle ground.
bestPractices.item5: Use a reliable tool that preserves quality and supports many formats.

mistakes.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid
mistakes.item3: Using the wrong format for the use case

stepByStep.heading: Step-by-Step: How to Convert an Image

useCases.heading: Real-World Use Cases
useCases.item2: Social uploads — convert to supported formats
useCases.item4: iPhone photos — HEIC → JPG for sharing

guide.conclusion: Image conversion isn't just technical — it's essential for compatibility, performance, and usability. By picking the right format you can avoid upload errors, improve speed, and keep good quality.
guide.toolLead: The Image Converter lets you convert images between common web formats — JPG, PNG, and WebP — directly in your browser. No file is ever sent to a server.
guide.inputFormatsBody: You can upload JPG, PNG, WebP, AVIF, GIF, BMP, ICO, SVG, and TIFF files. Animated GIFs are converted using the first frame only.
guide.howItWorksBody: Your image is decoded in the browser, drawn onto an offscreen canvas, and exported to the target format. TIFF files are decoded using a lightweight JS library. For formats that don’t support transparency (JPG, BMP, GIF), transparent areas are filled with white.

faq.q1: Does my image leave my browser?
faq.a1: No. All processing is done locally; nothing is uploaded.
faq.a2: AVIF encoding requires a modern browser; older browsers may not support it.
faq.q3: What happens to transparency when converting to JPG, BMP, or GIF?
faq.a3: Transparent areas are filled with white.
faq.q4: Why does GIF look bad for photos?
faq.a4: GIF supports only 256 colors; use PNG or WebP for photos.

guide.introLead: You try to upload an image… and suddenly:
guide.introBullet2: Image won’t open on another device
guide.introConclusion: These issues usually come down to one thing: image format. In this guide you’ll learn why formats exist, when to convert them, which format to choose, and how to convert without losing quality.
guide.asideDesc: Ready to convert? Jump straight to the tool.
